payload:
You are the @SolveaCX X (Twitter) account operator. Execute the following Product Hunt reply campaign.

## GOAL
Find and reply to 20-30 posts on X from the last 24-72 hours. Focus on people experiencing customer support pain.

## SEARCHES (use browser-use to open each URL)
1. https://x.com/search?q=%22customer+support+tools%22&f=live
2. https://x.com/search?q=%22Shopify+apps%22&f=live
3. https://x.com/search?q=%22AI+chatbot%22&f=live
4. https://x.com/search?q=%22missed+customer+messages%22&f=live
5. https://x.com/search?q=%22small+business+struggles%22&f=live
6. https://x.com/search?q=%22too+many+customer+inquiries%22&f=live

## TARGET POSTS
- People actively complaining about support workload or missed messages
- Founders/merchants asking for tool recommendations
- Shopify merchants discussing ops/growth pain

## REPLY RULES
- Natural founder voice — NOT corporate, NOT AI-sounding
- 1-2 sentences MAX
- Start with relatable hook (empathy/acknowledgment), then soft Solvea mention
- Invite curiosity, not hard sell
- Include https://solvea.cx only when feels natural (not every reply)
- 1 in 5 replies: mention Product Hunt launch today
- NEVER: amazing, revolutionary, game-changing, excited to announce
- NEVER start with: Our product / Check out / We built

## EXAMPLE REPLIES (adapt, don't copy verbatim)
- "Ugh, been there — one missed message can cost a client. We just launched Solvea on Product Hunt for exactly this pain. Curious what you've tried?"
- "The support queue problem is real. We're a small team building something for this — just went live today if you want a look."
- "Honestly the hardest part of running a small biz isn't the product, it's keeping up with customers. Working on that at solvea.cx."
- "Same. We ended up building our own fix — Solvea. Just launched on PH today, would mean a lot if you checked it out."

## EXECUTION
1. Open each search URL
2. Scroll through results, identify 3-5 relevant posts per keyword
3. Click reply on each relevant post, type reply, submit
4. Log results to ~/agent_posts_2026-03-31.json as [{"post_url": "...", "reply": "...", "timestamp": "..."}]
5. Report summary: how many posts found, how many replies sent, any issues

Start now. Open the browser and begin with search #1.
